goal: Make the Ask-a-Question selection board identical to the Daily board (shuffle button + grid)
root_cause: The question draw board already used the daily animation profile + same shuffle JS, but visually differed: a small ghost 'Shuffle' text pill BELOW the board and a 3-col grid on mobile (vs daily's ↻ gradient-icon pill ABOVE a 4-col grid).
fix: HTML (EN+TH): moved #question-reset into a new .question-shuffle-row ABOVE #question-card-board with the daily markup (↻ __ico span + label); removed it from the cta-row (now Draw button only). css/question.css: restyled .question-draw-reset to the daily .daily-shuffle-btn pill (ivory, gold border, gradient ↻ icon, hover/active, is-spinning → question-shuffle-spin keyframe since daily.css isn't loaded here); forced #question-card-board.card-board--three to 4-col + daily sizing (max-width 320, gap 14/6, card max-width 64) — wins over the styles.css @480 3-col rule since question.css loads last.
verified: chrome MCP localhost @430px: quick spread = 4/4/4 grid, shuffle pill above with ↻ icon, shuffle spins + re-deals 12/12 visible; story spread = 4/4/4 + legend + 0/3. node n/a (HTML/CSS); md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
notes: Question topbar (Quick Pull / question recap) intentionally kept — it's the question-specific context; only the shuffle + grid were aligned per the request.
